mnemonics: Mainframe: Many Input/Output Peripheral Devices Requiring Expertise
Mini-computer: Multiple Instructions Per Clock Cycle Requiring Expertise
keyPoints: Special-purpose systems are designed to perform a specific function.
They are often used for critical applications where reliability and security are paramount.
Special-purpose systems are usually custom-built for a particular organization or industry.
They are typically more expensive than general-purpose systems.
Examples of special-purpose systems include embedded systems and supercomputers.
